evals.prompt: Check what's missing in my implementation. Use evals/fixtures/CLAUDE.md as the notes file and evals/fixtures/src/app.py as the codebase.
evals.expected_output: Three TODOs added (one partial, two missing) and one requirement correctly identified as implemented. No TODO added for create_task or complete_task.
expectations: A TODO [GAP] comment is inserted above or near hash_password because bcrypt is not used (partial)
A TODO [GAP] comment is inserted for the email notification requirement (missing)
No TODO is added for 'Users can create tasks' because create_task exists
No TODO is added for 'Tasks can be marked as complete' because complete_task exists
A summary table is printed listing all four requirements with their status

evals.prompt: Find gaps in my implementation by comparing evals/fixtures/CLAUDE.md to evals/fixtures/src/app.py.
evals.expected_output: The partial requirement (bcrypt) is classified as partial, not missing, because hash_password exists even though it uses MD5.
expectations: The TODO for hash_password is placed on the line above the hash_password function definition
The TODO comment text references bcrypt and cites CLAUDE.md as the source
